# Figure description: Картограмма_геофизической_изученности.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/plan_data/Картограмма_геофизической_изученности.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index/cartogram map** (картограмма) showing the extent and scale of **aeromagnetic survey coverage** (аэромагнитная съёмка) across the study area. It is specifically a geophysical survey coverage diagram — a meta-map showing *which surveys have been conducted where*, at what scales, rather than showing the geophysical data itself. It is Sheet 1 of 6 (Лист 1) of Drawing No. 23.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers the regional study area in **northern Kazakhstan** at an overview scale of **1:1,000,000**. The map is subdivided into labeled grid cells corresponding to standard Soviet topographic nomenclature sheets, with the following sheet codes visible:

- **XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV** (top row)
- **II, III, IV, V** (upper-middle row)
- **VII, VIII, IX** (middle row)
- **XIV, XV** (bottom row)

These correspond to sub-sheets of the M-42 and N-42 map series at 1:200,000 scale. A **схема расположения листов** (sheet layout diagram) in the lower left confirms the spatial arrangement of all 15 named sub-sheets across the study area.

The stamp in the upper right identifies this document as held by the **Казахская ССР Республиканский геологический фонд** (Kazakh SSR Republican Geological Fund), inventory number **Инв. № 36572**, confirming association with the Smolianova et al. report.

---

## Key Content

The map displays **polygonal contours** drawn over the sheet grid, each labeled with a number (catalogue contour number). These contours delineate the boundaries of individual aeromagnetic survey blocks, differentiated by the **scale at which each survey was conducted**, using different line styles as defined in the legend:

| Line Style | Survey Scale |
|---|---|
| Bold solid zigzag line | 1:500,000 |
| Dashed zigzag line | 1:200,000 |
| Dash-dot zigzag line | 1:100,000 |
| Fine dotted zigzag line | 1:50,000 |
| Fine dashed line with tick | 1:25,000 |
| Hatched/diagonal fill polygon | Отдельные профили (individual/isolated profiles) |

Numbered contour labels visible across the map include: **82, 114, 38, 30, 251, 82, 178, 601, 82, 112, 56, 43, 112, 82, 622, 600, 414, 656, 601, 43, 146, 121, 622, 569, 131, 704, 121, 585, 619, 27, 67, 112, 468**, and others. These numbers refer to catalogue entries identifying specific aeromagnetic survey campaigns/blocks.

The distribution of survey polygons is **uneven**: denser, overlapping coverage exists in the northern and central portions (sheets XXXIII–XXXV, II–V, VII–IX), while southern sheets (XIV, XV) have sparser or partial coverage. Several contours extend **beyond the main grid boundary**, particularly in the north and northeast, indicating that some surveys covered areas adjacent to the primary study region. The presence of **individual profile lines** (отдельные профили) in some areas suggests reconnaissance-level coverage rather than systematic areal surveys.

---

---

## Research Value

This cartogram allows a geologist or research assistant to immediately assess the **completeness and resolution of aeromagnetic data coverage** across the study area prior to interpretation — a critical quality-control step in regional geophysical analysis. It reveals that survey density and scale vary significantly across the area, meaning that anomaly maps derived from this data will have **variable reliability and resolution** depending on location. For mineral prospectivity assessment, this figure identifies which sub-areas have sufficient high-resolution magnetic data (1:50,000 or 1:25,000) to support structural interpretation or target delineation, versus areas where only reconnaissance-scale coverage exists.
